questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq belonged to which dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq was a Sultan of the Tughlaq dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: During which years did Muhammad bin Tughlaq rule Delhi?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq ruled from 1325 to 1351 CE.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq is most famous for his attempt to shift the capital from Delhi to where?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq famously tried to move the capital from Delhi to Daulatabad in the Deccan.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's currency experiment involved introducing what kind of coinage?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq introduced a token currency of base metal meant to circulate at the value of silver.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq is generally remembered in history as a ruler full of what?
questions.explanation: He is remembered for bold and ambitious schemes that often ended in failure.

questions.q: Daulatabad, the planned new capital, was located in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Daulatabad lay in the Deccan, far to the south of Delhi.

questions.q: From which city did Muhammad bin Tughlaq primarily rule the Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Delhi was the principal seat of Muhammad bin Tughlaq's Sultanate, despite his attempt to relocate the capital.

questions.q: The token currency scheme of Muhammad bin Tughlaq ultimately ended in what?
questions.explanation: The token currency was widely forged and had to be withdrawn at great cost, ending in failure.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's reign falls within which broad era of Indian history?
questions.explanation: He ruled during the Delhi Sultanate period, which lasted from 1206 to 1526 CE.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq was the son and successor of which Sultan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq succeeded his father, Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq, the founder of the dynasty.

questions.q: What was Muhammad bin Tughlaq's name before he became Sultan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq was known as Juna Khan before he ascended the throne in 1325.

questions.q: Why did Muhammad bin Tughlaq want to move the capital to Daulatabad?
options: It was free of Mongol attack only
It was more central to his expanded empire
questions.explanation: Daulatabad was chosen as a more central location to govern his greatly extended empire in the south.

questions.q: The famous Moroccan traveller who visited and served at Muhammad bin Tughlaq's court was whom?
questions.explanation: Ibn Battuta visited Delhi during Muhammad bin Tughlaq's reign and served as a qazi (judge) at his court.

questions.q: What was the original name of the city of Daulatabad before it was renamed?
questions.explanation: Daulatabad was the former Yadava capital of Devagiri, renamed by the Tughlaqs.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's token coins were largely made of which metals?
questions.explanation: The token currency consisted of copper and brass coins meant to pass at the value of silver tankas.

questions.q: A chief reason the token currency failed was that:
options: It was too heavy to carry
People forged it easily at home
questions.explanation: Because the coins were cheap base metal, people forged them in large numbers, destroying their value.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq's planned expedition toward Khurasan and Central Asia ended how?
options: It was abandoned after huge expense
questions.explanation: He raised a vast army for a Khurasan campaign but disbanded it after enormous cost without achieving anything.

questions.q: The ill-fated expedition Muhammad bin Tughlaq sent toward the Himalayas (often called the Qarachil campaign) ended in what?
questions.explanation: The Qarachil expedition into the mountains ended in disaster, with most of the army destroyed.

questions.q: Which two important southern kingdoms emerged or asserted independence during the turmoil of Muhammad bin Tughlaq's reign?
questions.explanation: During his troubled reign, both the Vijayanagara Empire (1336) and the Bahmani Sultanate (1347) arose in the south.

questions.q: What was a major human consequence of forcing Delhi's population to migrate to Daulatabad?
options: Great hardship and many deaths on the road
questions.explanation: The forced migration over a long distance caused immense suffering and many deaths, and Delhi was left depopulated.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq eventually did what regarding the capital shift to Daulatabad?
options: Reversed it and returned the capital to Delhi
questions.explanation: After great loss, Muhammad bin Tughlaq abandoned the scheme and shifted the capital back to Delhi.

questions.q: Who succeeded Muhammad bin Tughlaq as Sultan after his death in 1351?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq was succeeded by his cousin Firuz Shah Tughlaq in 1351.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq tried to raise revenue by sharply increasing taxation in which fertile region, triggering revolt?
options: The Doab (between the Ganga and Yamuna)
questions.explanation: He raised taxes heavily in the Doab during a famine, provoking widespread revolt among the peasantry.

questions.q: Muhammad bin Tughlaq sought formal legitimacy by obtaining recognition from which authority?
questions.explanation: He sought and received a robe of investiture from the Abbasid caliph residing in Cairo to legitimise his rule.

questions.q: The Vijayanagara Empire that arose during his reign was founded by which brothers?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara was founded in 1336 by the brothers Harihara and Bukka amid the breakup of Tughlaq authority in the south.

questions.q: Ibn Battuta records that Muhammad bin Tughlaq sent him as an envoy to which country?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq dispatched Ibn Battuta as an ambassador to the Mongol court of China, a journey Ibn Battuta later described.

questions.q: Despite his failed schemes, Muhammad bin Tughlaq was personally known for what intellectual quality?
options: Deep learning in philosophy and sciences
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq was unusually learned, versed in logic, philosophy, mathematics, medicine, and astronomy.

questions.q: Where did Muhammad bin Tughlaq die in 1351, while campaigning against rebels?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq died in 1351 in Sindh, near Thatta, while pursuing a rebel.

questions.q: The historian and contemporary who wrote critically of Muhammad bin Tughlaq's reign in the Tarikh-i-Firuz Shahi was whom?
questions.explanation: Ziauddin Barani, a courtier, chronicled and criticised Muhammad bin Tughlaq's policies in his Tarikh-i-Firuz Shahi.

questions.q: Modern assessments often describe Muhammad bin Tughlaq as which paradox?
options: A 'mixture of opposites' or wise fool
questions.explanation: Historians often call him a 'mixture of opposites'—brilliant and visionary yet impractical, his good ideas undone by harsh execution.
